bio.quote: Hild is a Dwarf innkeeper who runs the Broken Stair Inn — the only inn in Stairwatch. She asks no questions, charges fair prices, and waters the ale 'just enough to be annoying.' She knows Lew well —

system.description:
Dwarves

Stereotype: Greedy as a dragon, stubborn as a glacier.

Size: 1M; Speed: 5; Resistance: 0

novice.description:
Dwarves

Stereotype: Greedy as a dragon, stubborn as a glacier.

Size: 1M; Speed: 5; Resistance: 0

system.description: You can paint a rune onto your skin (10 min work). Only one active at a time. * *Detection:* Glows near chosen creature/object type (20 sq). * *Light:* Sheds light (10 sq). Toggle as free action. * *Voice:* Rune-echo bond with one willing creature (1 mile; functions as a telepathic bond).

novice.description: You can paint a rune onto your skin (10 min work). Only one active at a time. * *Detection:* Glows near chosen creature/object type (20 sq). * *Light:* Sheds light (10 sq). Toggle as free action. * *Voice:* Rune-echo bond with one willing creature (1 mile; functions as a telepathic bond).

system.description: You gain an Advantage (Second Chance) on Reason or Intuition tests to identify structural weaknesses or history in stone buildings.

novice.description: You gain an Advantage (Second Chance) on Reason or Intuition tests to identify structural weaknesses or history in stone buildings.

system.description:
A bog-iron and peat community built on stilts above hungry ground, with seasonal movement between safe platforms and hard-learned routes.

Language: Myrmål

Environment: Rural; Organization: Communal; Upbringing: Labor

novice.description:

Language: Myrmål

system.description:
Skills: Cunning, Handle Animals, Crafting.

Languages: No additional languages.

Renown: 0; Wealth: 0; Project Points: 200

Perk: *Practical Stores*. Once per extended rest, you can declare you packed one mundane supply item that fits your travel (food, cord, oil, simple tools).

Languages: No additional languages.

system.description: You can use the Handle Animals skill to interact with nonsapient creatures who are not animals.

novice.description: You can use the Handle Animals skill to interact with nonsapient creatures who are not animals.

system.description: The whole world’s a stage, and everyone on it, an actor. No one knows this better than the troubadour. You find energy in the drama of everyday life and know how to draw spectacle forth from even the most mundane of situations. You accent highs and deepen lows in service to whoever might witness your performance. As a troubadour, you chase drama. The insurmountable dangers of the world might cause many a hero to cower. But you take to that world stage not intending to die, but to find out if you are truly alive

But you take to that world stage not intending to die, but to find out if you are truly alive

system.description:
During battles, you are fueled by the dynamic ups, downs, and upside downs of the fray, from which you derive a Heroic Resource called drama.

Drama in Combat

At the start of a combat encounter or some other stressful situation tracked in combat rounds (as determined by the Director), you gain drama equal to your Milestones. At the start of each of your turns during combat, you gain 1d3 drama. Additionally, you gain drama when certain events occur during a combat encounter:

  • The first time three or more heroes use an ability on the same turn, you gain 2 drama.

  • The first time any hero is made winded during the encounter, you gain 2 drama.

  • Whenever a creature within your line of effect rolls a natural 19 or 20, you gain 3 drama.

  • When you or another hero dies, you gain 10 drama.

When you are dead, you continue to gain drama during combat as long as your body is intact. If you have 30 drama during the encounter in which you died, you can come back to life with 1 Toughness and 0 drama (no action required). If you are still dead after the encounter in which you died, you can’t gain drama during future encounters.

You lose any remaining drama at the end of the encounter.

Drama Outside of Combat

Though you can’t gain drama outside of combat, you can use your heroic abilities and effects that cost drama without spending it. Whenever you use an ability or effect outside of combat that costs drama, you can’t use that same ability or effect outside of combat again until you earn 1 or more Milestones or finish a extended rest.

When you use an ability outside of combat that lets you spend unlimited drama on its effect, such as Artful Flourish, you can use it as if you had spent an amount of drama equal to your Milestones.

system.description:
You enter every battle with a set of performance abilities at the ready. Performances are magical presentations (such as songs, dances, poems, or gymnastic feats) that your allies can participate in. These abilities have the Persuasive keyword. At the start of each combat round, as long as you are not dazed, dead, or surprised, you can either choose a new performance or maintain your current performance (no action required). Your performance lasts until you are unable to maintain it or until the end of the encounter.

You start off with the Choreography and Revitalizing Limerick performance abilities. Your choice of class act grants you additional performances.

These abilities have the Persuasive keyword. At the start of each combat round, as long as you are not dazed, dead, or surprised, you can either choose a new performance or maintain your current performance (no action required). Your performance lasts until you are unable to maintain it or until the end of the encounter.

You start off with the Choreography and Revitalizing Limerick performance abilities. Your choice of class act grants you additional performances.

system.description:
Whenever you obtain a success on a test to interact with an NPC using a skill from the interpersonal group, you can form a bond with that NPC. When you enter into a negotiation with a bonded NPC, their patience increases by 1 (to a maximum of 5). Additionally, the first time during a negotiation that you personally make an argument that would increase a bonded NPC’s interest by 1, you instead increase their interest by 2 (to a maximum of 5).

You can have a number of bonds active equal to your level. When you form a bond with a new NPC that would exceed the limit, you must choose which of your active bonds to lose.

system.description: Providing throwable light weapons and light armor easily concealed by a cloak to confuse your enemies, the Cloak and Dagger kit makes you more mobile while increasing the effectiveness of your short-range strikes.

novice.description: Providing throwable light weapons and light armor easily concealed by a cloak to confuse your enemies, the Cloak and Dagger kit makes you more mobile while increasing the effectiveness of your short-range strikes.

system.description: Drama infuses your every movement done in tandem with another. You perform dances of death, putting trust in your opponent to return your passion in kind. You have the Gymnastics skill.

novice.description: Drama infuses your every movement done in tandem with another. You perform dances of death, putting trust in your opponent to return your passion in kind. You have the Gymnastics skill.
